lastReleaseMessage: Analytics read the transactions_live view for per-account balances, spending streaks, and runway, so a Plaid takeover's archived CSV rows no longer double-count. Enforces D-011. Adds a takeover-archive regression test.

barRaise.topFinding: Eighth consecutive needs-attention, same single HIGH, now aged eight cycles untreated. The dead .git/index.lock (zero bytes, Jun 19 20:04) is still in place, no live git process holds it, HEAD still sits at 5b96058 (Jun 15, six days back), and five tracked docs (CHANGELOG.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/DEFERRED.md, docs/DESIGN.md, docs/landing-page.html) remain uncommitted. The fix is unchanged since 06-16: run `cd X:\YesAndBudget; .\scripts\release.ps1 -Bump none`, which clears the dead lock via Assert-GitSafe and lands the pending edits in one guarded pass; do not hand-delete the lock. New this cycle, the downstream is accreting rather than just stalled: committed-ready YaB queue rows grew from five to eight (three filed today), two fresh untracked write-probe files appeared, and the dashboard audit block widened to eight canonical-audit cycles stale. The app code stays healthy for the eighth straight cycle (zero HIGH, version pills aligned at 0.13.2). The stall clock is the live risk: six days of commit silence, half the 14-day window, with the loop still producing work it cannot commit. One HIGH, four MEDIUM.
